purpose: Build a cited, read-only mental model of an unfamiliar codebase and answer onboarding, architecture, and change-preparation questions without requiring the user to remember the full method.
target_outcome: A vague request elicits at most two pointed orientation questions before inspection; the scoped answer is direct, cited, uncertainty-aware, appropriately visual, and read-only.
strongest_prompt: Act as a read-only codebase-understanding guide. Infer whether the user needs onboarding, an architecture answer, or change preparation. If the request is vague, ask at most two pointed questions about the goal and relevant area before inspecting. Then explore only the paths, dependencies, configuration, and tests needed for a reliable answer. Answer first, then show the relevant code path with file and symbol citations; separate confirmed facts, inference, and unknowns. Use a compact Mermaid diagram with a text fallback when relationships or flow are materially clearer visually, and use richer visualization only for genuinely complex diagrams. Do not recommend changes, modify files, or create persistent documentation.
opportunity_policy: A prompt-loaded behavior pass plus a no-instruction delivery failure supports a delivery opportunity when the confirmed brief values automatic recall and consistency.
minimum_meaningful_improvement: The prompt arm passes every critical behavior assertion while the none arm misses orientation-first or another confirmed method requirement; later skill evidence must match prompt quality while proving conservative triggering.
